---
description: "Use when updating README, analysis markdown files, or notebook writeups for this COVID-19 data project. Enforces assignment-style numbered sections, clear methodology, and Quick Start commands."
name: "Hackathon Documentation Style"
applyTo: "**/*.md"
---
# Hackathon Documentation Style

Use these conventions for project documentation updates.

## Structure

- Keep sections aligned to assignment steps when applicable (for example: 1.1 to 1.5, 2.1 to 2.5).
- Include clear section headers for: objective, dataset, workflow, outputs, and limitations.
- Keep wording practical and concise; avoid vague filler.

## README Requirements

- Include a one-paragraph project summary at the top.
- Include a `Quick Start` section with runnable environment and launch commands.
- Document repository structure with one-line purpose for each key file.
- Document core metrics and how they are calculated.
- Include interpretation caveats so rankings are not overclaimed.

## Analysis Writeups

- For conclusion files, use explicit numbered headings when requested (for example: 7.1 Conclusions, 7.2 Limitations, 7.3 Next Steps).
- Separate findings from limitations and future work.
- Keep claims tied to produced tables/charts rather than assumptions.

## Formatting

- Use Markdown tables only when they improve readability over bullets.
- Prefer short lists and direct wording.
- Keep command examples copy-paste ready for Windows PowerShell unless asked otherwise.
